title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: 'No Kings' protests occurred in Madrid on March 28, 2026, featuring anti-Donald Trump placards.
The 'No Kings' movement held over 3,000 protests across the U.S., with major events in Massachusetts.
Demonstrations spread internationally, with large events in Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam supporting the U.S. protests.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres on Bravo April 2 at 9 p.m., set in Providence and Cranston.
Cast member Alicia Carmody runs Pizza Mamma in Cranston; Liz McGraw owns cannabis dispensary The Slater Center in Providence.
The show explores dynamics among Rhode Islanders with deep community roots, featuring friendships, feuds, and shifting loyalties.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff will cut around 50 jobs to save $4 million after a $26.5 million budget reduction.
Reductions will impact police presence and response times in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, and Palmer.
The cuts are due to structural funding issues outlined by the Office of the Inspector General, not department overspending.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy approved $2.65 million to replace firefighters' gear after tests showed PFAS, linked to cancer, in old equipment.
The city will purchase two PFAS-free protective gear sets for each of its over 270 firefighters in the coming weeks.
In January, Massachusetts will prohibit the sale of firefighting gear with intentionally added PFAS chemicals.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley discusses the 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary at Concord Carlisle Regional High School on March 23.
The Walden Woods Project, founded by Henley in 1990, aims to preserve Thoreau's land and legacy in Concord.
The documentary, featuring narrations by George Clooney and others, airs on PBS on March 30 and 31.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics enjoyed a team outing at Gillette Stadium to watch the France vs. Brazil friendly soccer match.
France defeated Brazil 2-1, with a goal from star forward Kylian Mbappe, impressing Celtics players in attendance.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla noted strategic parallels between basketball and soccer during the team's bonding experience.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 win over the Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed with 25 points, 12 rebounds, and five assists despite struggling with his shot.
Coach Mazzulla confirmed Jaylen Brown, dealing with Achilles tendinitis, is considered day-to-day.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after a ruptured Achilles, despite having the option to take more recovery time.
Tatum cited the Celtics' strong championship contention and not wanting to miss the opportunity as key reasons for his comeback.
In his return, Tatum is contributing with 9.2 rebounds per game, although his shooting percentages have yet to reach career averages.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed after a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard Friday afternoon.
The Middlesex DA’s Office reports the shooting followed an altercation between two armed men who knew each other.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex DA’s Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 under new ownership by the Fertitta family.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the city's former WNBA team disbanded in 2008.
The purchase price of the Connecticut Sun is reportedly a WNBA record $300 million.
